summary: Funding-neutral OI pump with extreme taker buying — all novel_dim fields are at-T0 observable (taker_buy_ratio_5m, funding, oi_change_pct), no leakage; concentration and live-overlap with existing pump-long strategy warrant monitoring but not blocking.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: All four novel_dim fields are evaluated at T0: taker_buy_ratio_5m is a trailing 5-minute aggregate (backward-looking), funding is the current funding rate snapshot, and oi_change_pct is the OI burst that defines the signal. No 'forward_*', 'confirm_after_*', or post-T0 suffix detected. Field names are in the SUPPORTED_FIELDS set per the R1 bug fix.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Channel=OI_Price, side=long directly overlaps with the user's live BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump-long strategy. The funding-neutral filter (|funding|<=1e-4) plus taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 plus oi_change_pct>=10 will produce a strict subset of triggers that the live system also fires on — same-side same-symbol stacking, not opposing trades. At $1000 capital this is paper-shadow monitoring, not a blocker.
risk_assessments.remediation: Paper-shadow only: tag proposals on OI_Price/long with a 'live_overlap=BWE_OI_Price_pump_long' annotation in the registry so downstream sizing logic can de-duplicate against the live strategy before any capital allocation. Track concurrent-trigger count vs. live signal in the shadow sim; if oi_change_pct>=10 + funding-neutral + taker>=0.65 fires on a narrow symbol set (e.g. >40% of triggers concentrated in 1–2 tickers), demote to 'caution-hold' before promotion. No code change required for the proposal itself.
